div1.entryFree:
Variable, 1) changeable, inconstant: lest that thy love prove likewise v. Rom. II, 2, 111.
2) changing, various, different: v. passions throng her constant woe, Ven. 967. I never heard a passion so confused, so strange, so outrageous, and so v. Merch. II, 8, 13. ridges horsed with v. complexions, Cor. II, 1, 228. countries different with v. objects, Hml. III, 1, 180. your fat king und your lean beggar is but v. service, IV, 3, 25. whiles he is vaulting v. ramps, Cymb. I, 6, 134.
